texto: He looked at the little maiden, and she looked at him; and he felt that he was melting away, but he still managed to keep himself erect, shouldering his gun bravely. A door was suddenly opened, the draught caught the little dancer and she fluttered like a sylph, straight into the fire, to the soldier, blazed up and was gone! By this time the soldier was reduced to a mere lump, and when the maid took away the ashes next morning she found him, in the shape of a small tin heart. All that was left of the dancer was her spangle, and that was burnt as black as a coal.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hans Christian Andersen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He looked at the little maiden, and she looked at him; and he felt that he was melting away, but …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, fairytale, hans-christian-andersen pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e hans-christian-andersen.

texto: I can give her no greater power than she has already, said the woman; don't you see how strong that is? How men and animals are obliged to serve her, and how well she has got through the world, barefooted as she is. She cannot receive any power from me greater than she now has, which consists in her own purity and innocence of heart. If she cannot herself obtain access to the Snow Queen, and remove the glass fragments from little Kay, we can do nothing to help her.
